publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Data-structure choice; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Data-structure choice: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? One structure, such as a list, is always best because it can represent every small dataset somehow.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. List the dominant operations and invariants first, compare at least one alternative, and choose the simplest structure whose guarantees match those needs.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. List the dominant operations and invariants first, compare at least one alternative, and choose the simplest structure whose guarantees match those needs.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Data-structure choice instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: List the dominant operations and invariants first, compare at least one alternative, and choose the simplest structure whose guarantees match those needs.
One structure, such as a list, is always best because it can represent every small dataset somehow.
